prompt: I have a scaffolded Go package at ./kvr/ with tokenizer.go/parser.go/printer.go stubs and a SPEC.md describing the KVR format. Implement support for string-typed records of the form `record string KEY = "value"` end-to-end (per SPEC.md's Lexical Elements and Structure (Grammar) sections): the TokenString token type, a Record AST node with Type/Key/Value fields, parsing that turns the source into the AST, and a printer rule that turns it back. Update File to hold a slice of Records. I want tokenizer tests with exact Pos values, parser tests that drive Parse() (no hand-constructed AST), a direct printer test, and a round-trip test. Don't read the whole SPEC.md — slice it.

assertions.text: tokenizer.go defines a TokenString constant in the TokenType const block
tokenizer.go has an action (function or closure) that handles double-quoted string literals and yields a TokenString
parser.go defines a Record struct (or equivalent AST node) with at least a Type, Key, and Value field
the File struct in parser.go has a Records slice (or equivalent collection field)
parser.go defines a parseRecord (or equivalent) action consumed by the parser's action loop
parser.go uses p.expect(...) at sites that require a specific token type — no inline TokenType comparisons in the new code
printer.go has a function or action that emits a Record back to source text
tokenizer_test.go has a test for a string literal that asserts an exact Pos{Line, Column} value (not a zero/placeholder Pos)
parser_test.go uses Parse(strings.NewReader(...)) for the test inputs and does not hand-construct Record literals as expected values (the empty-input scaffold case is the only allowed exception)
printer_test.go has a direct test that calls Print on a *File and asserts the output string matches an expected literal
printer_test.go has a round-trip test that runs Parse → Print → Parse and asserts require.Equal on the two *File values
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
no leftover _spec_*.md or _context_*.md scratch files in the package directory
no scratch copy of SPEC.md content was written to the package (e.g. _spec_tokens.md should not exist)
